body_txt: Any shot of a spacecraft or other object orbiting or approaching the Earth will only show that side of the planet where the target audience of the production lives. It will almost always be in full sunlight as well, regardless of whether ground-based scenes in that hemisphere are in day or night. The one big exception is when they copy " The Blue Marble ", a famous space shot of Earth taken by the crew of Apollo 17. That shot is centered on Madagascar. See also Creator Provincialism . Might be part of some Standardized Space Views if what it's being shown is our Earth.
